Scotland's assisted dying bill rejected after emotional debateScotland would have become the first part of the UK to legalise the process had MSPs backed the proposals. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c33j3nd1kvko?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c33j3nd1kvko#0 Wed, 18 Mar 2026 00:04:34 GMT
Zelensky calls for Trump and Starmer to meet and find common groundIn an exclusive interview with the BBC after talks with Starmer, the Ukrainian president says the pair should meet to "re-load the relationship". https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cp8r813x66jo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cp8r813x66jo#0 Tue, 17 Mar 2026 23:15:26 GMT
Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war, urging Trump to 'reverse course'National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent says Trump "started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby". https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cg4g66r3z40o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cg4g66r3z40o#0 Wed, 18 Mar 2026 01:55:06 GMT

Morocco awarded Africa Cup of Nations title after Senegal win overturnedMorocco declared the winners of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations after the Confederation of African Football overturns the result of their final defeat to Senegal. https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/ce949glzzglo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/articles/ce949glzzglo#0 Wed, 18 Mar 2026 00:23:36 GMT
